title: MULTI-CLARID (Multimodal Category Learning and Resting-state Imaging Data)
description: MULTI-CLARID is a multimodal neuroimaging dataset combining simultaneous fMRI and EEG recordings during an auditory category learning task and resting-state fMRI. The dataset includes 63-channel EEG with physiological monitoring (ECG, EOG, EMG, skin conductance), structural MRI (T1-weighted and PD-weighted UTE), and behavioral measures. Participants learned to classify frequency-modulated sounds based on binary acoustic features through trial-and-error feedback over approximately 27 minutes, providing a rich resource for investigating neural mechanisms of category learning and multimodal brain imaging integration.
methods_description: Data were acquired using a 3 Tesla Philips Achieva dStream MRI scanner with 32-channel head coil. EEG was recorded at 5 kHz using 64 channels (including ECG) with BrainAmp MRplus amplifiers, supplemented by 16-channel bipolar recordings for EOG, EMG, and artifact correction. Respiration and heart rate were recorded at 100 Hz. Auditory stimuli were presented via MR-compatible headphones. The category learning task comprised 180 trials with pseudo-randomized presentation of frequency-modulated sounds varying in duration, frequency modulation direction, intensity, speed, and frequency range. Participants indicated category membership via button press and received immediate or delayed feedback. Resting-state fMRI was acquired for 10 minutes with eyes closed.
